# CLAUDE.md Compliance Checklist

**Purpose:** Ensure every development task follows the mandatory CLAUDE.md workflow
**Usage:** Reference this checklist BEFORE starting ANY coding task

---

## Pre-Flight Checklist

### Before Starting ANY Task

- [ ] **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-11)
- [ ] **Sync with main branch:** `git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit`
- [ ] **Create task file:** `tasks/[feature-name].md` using template from `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- [ ] **Fill out task plan:**
  - Problem statement
  - Files to read
  - Proposed changes
  - Todo checklist with estimates
- [ ] **Get user approval** via `ExitPlanMode` tool (Rule #3)
- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin implementation

---

## During Implementation

### As You Work

- [ ] **Update todos** as you complete each item (Rule #2)
- [ ] **Make changes as simple as possible** (Rule #9)
  - Impact minimal code
  - Avoid complex refactors unless necessary
  - Keep changes focused on the task
- [ ] **Give high-level explanations** of changes (Rule #5)
- [ ] **No temporary fixes** - Find and fix root causes (Rule #8)
- [ ] **Follow privacy/security/UX principles** (CLAUDE.md Core Principles)

---

## When Complete

### Before Marking Work "Done"

- [ ] **Add review section** to `tasks/[feature].md` (Rule #7)
  - Summary of changes
  - Files created/modified
  - Testing status
  - Issues encountered
  - Time comparison (estimated vs actual)
- [ ] **Update documentation** if architecture changed (Rule #10)
  - `docs/technical/` for new systems/patterns
  - `docs/product/feature_map.md` for new features
  - Any other relevant docs
- [ ] **Run tests:** `npm run test`
- [ ] **Type check:** `npm run type-check`
- [ ] **Lint check:** `npm run lint`
- [ ] **Build check:** `npm run build` (verify bundle size < 100KB)
- [ ] **Verify in light mode**
- [ ] **Verify in dark mode**
- [ ] **Test keyboard navigation**
- [ ] **Test at different screen sizes** (375px, 768px, 1024px)

---

## Git Commit Checklist

### Before Creating Commit

- [ ] **Stage task file:** `git add tasks/[feature].md` (pre-commit hook will block if missing)
- [ ] **Review changes:** `git diff --staged`
- [ ] **Write clear commit message:**
  ```
  type: Short description (50 chars max)

  - Bullet point details
  - What changed and why
  - Related issues (#123)

  Generated with Claude Code
  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
  ```
- [ ] **Commit types:** feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, chore

---

## Rules #1-11 Quick Reference

### CLAUDE.md Core Rules (IMMUTABLE)

1. **Think вҶ’ Plan вҶ’ Write to tasks/todo.md** - Create task file FIRST
2. **Plan has todo list** - Checklist with items to check off
3. **Check with me before working** - Get approval via ExitPlanMode
4. **Begin working, mark tasks complete** - Update todos as you go
5. **High-level explanations only** - Don't overexplain every detail
6. **Make changes as simple as possible** - Minimal code impact
7. **Add review section** when done - Document what changed
8. **DO NOT BE LAZY** - Find root cause, no temporary fixes
9. **Simplicity is key** - Impact minimal code, avoid bugs
10. **Update documentation** - Keep docs detailed, clear, clean
11. **Sync with main** at start of EVERY session - Stay up to date

---

## Automation Tools

### Available Helpers

- **Git Pre-Commit Hook:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
  - Blocks commits when code changes without task file updates
  - Ensures compliance with Rule #1-2
  - Can bypass with `--no-verify` (emergencies only)

- **Task Template:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
  - Standard format for all task files
  - Includes review section template
  - Maps to all CLAUDE.md rules

- **Custom Commands:** `.claude/commands/`
  - `/start-task` - Reminder to follow workflow
  - Add more as needed

- **Automation Guide:** `.claude/automation-guide.md`
  - Detailed setup instructions
  - Troubleshooting tips
  - Customization guide

---

## Emergency Bypass

### When to Override

Use `git commit --no-verify` ONLY for:
- Hotfix deployments
- Documentation-only changes
- Reverting broken commits
- Emergencies (site down, critical bug)

**Otherwise:** Always follow the full checklist.

---

## Questions?

- See: `CLAUDE.md` for full workflow guide
- See: `.claude/automation-guide.md` for automation help
- See: `tasks/` directory for example task files

---

**Remember:** These rules exist to ensure:
- Quality code
- Complete documentation
- No forgotten tasks
- Consistent workflow
- Better collaboration

**Following this checklist = Better code + Happier users** вң…

1вҶ’# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’**Version:** 1.0
     4вҶ’**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
     5вҶ’**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’---
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’## Table of Contents
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’1. [Overview](#overview)
    12вҶ’2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
    13вҶ’3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
    14вҶ’4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
    15вҶ’5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
    16вҶ’6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
    17вҶ’7. [Customization](#customization)
    18вҶ’8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’---
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’## Overview
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md rules #1-11 by:
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’вң… **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
    27вҶ’вң… **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
    28вҶ’вң… **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
    29вҶ’вң… **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’---
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’## Quick Start
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’### For Developers
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’1. **Before starting any task:**
    40вҶ’   ```bash
    41вҶ’   # Check workflow checklist
    42вҶ’   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
    43вҶ’   ```
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’2. **Create task file from template:**
    46вҶ’   ```bash
    47вҶ’   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
    48вҶ’   ```
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’3. **Git hook automatically enforces:**
    51вҶ’   - Task file must be staged when committing code changes
    52вҶ’   - Will block commit if missing
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’### For Claude Code
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’1. **Use `/start-task` command** (if available)
    57вҶ’2. **Reference workflow checklist** before starting
    58вҶ’3. **Use task template** for all new tasks
    59вҶ’4. **Git hook will catch** if task file missing
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’---
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’## Automation Tools
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
    68вҶ’**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
    69вҶ’**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’**How it works:**
    72вҶ’1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
    73вҶ’2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
    74вҶ’3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
    75вҶ’4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
    78вҶ’```
    79вҶ’в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
    80вҶ’  вқҢ COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
    81вҶ’в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’Changed code files:
    86вҶ’  - src/components/Modal.tsx
    87вҶ’  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
    88вҶ’
    89вҶ’Required Action:
    90вҶ’  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
    91вҶ’  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
    92вҶ’  3. Retry commit: git commit
    93вҶ’```
    94вҶ’
    95вҶ’**Emergency Bypass:**
    96вҶ’```bash
    97вҶ’git commit --no-verify
    98вҶ’```
    99вҶ’вҡ пёҸ **Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’---
   102вҶ’
   103вҶ’### 2. Workflow Checklist
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’**Location:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   106вҶ’**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
   107вҶ’**Enforces:** All rules #1-11
   108вҶ’
   109вҶ’**When to use:**
   110вҶ’- Before starting ANY task
   111вҶ’- During implementation (reference as needed)
   112вҶ’- Before marking work complete
   113вҶ’- Before creating git commits
   114вҶ’
   115вҶ’**Sections:**
   116вҶ’- вң… Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
   117вҶ’- вң… During Implementation
   118вҶ’- вң… When Complete
   119вҶ’- вң… Git Commit Checklist
   120вҶ’- вң… Rules #1-11 Quick Reference
   121вҶ’
   122вҶ’**View checklist:**
   123вҶ’```bash
   124вҶ’cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   125вҶ’```
   126вҶ’
   127вҶ’---
   128вҶ’
   129вҶ’### 3. Task Template
   130вҶ’
   131вҶ’**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   132вҶ’**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
   133вҶ’**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’**Sections included:**
   136вҶ’- Overview and problem statement
   137вҶ’- Files to read
   138вҶ’- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
   139вҶ’- Todo checklist with time estimates
   140вҶ’- Design considerations
   141вҶ’- Implementation notes
   142вҶ’- Testing checklist
   143вҶ’- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’**Create new task:**
   146вҶ’```bash
   147вҶ’cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   148вҶ’```
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’**Good naming examples:**
   151вҶ’- вң… `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
   152вҶ’- вң… `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
   153вҶ’- вң… `tasks/notes-ux-improvements.md`
   154вҶ’
   155вҶ’**Bad naming examples:**
   156вҶ’- вқҢ `tasks/todo.md`
   157вҶ’- вқҢ `tasks/task1.md`
   158вҶ’- вқҢ `tasks/new.md`
   159вҶ’
   160вҶ’---
   161вҶ’
   162вҶ’### 4. Custom Commands
   163вҶ’
   164вҶ’**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
   165вҶ’**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow reminders
   166вҶ’
   167вҶ’#### `/start-task` Command
   168вҶ’
   169вҶ’**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
   170вҶ’**Purpose:** Comprehensive reminder before starting any task
   171вҶ’
   172вҶ’**What it covers:**
   173вҶ’1. Sync with main branch (Rule #11)
   174вҶ’2. Read CLAUDE.md (Rules #1-11)
   175вҶ’3. Create task file from template
   176вҶ’4. Fill out task plan
   177вҶ’5. Get user approval (Rule #3)
   178вҶ’6. Implementation workflow
   179вҶ’7. Review section and documentation updates
   180вҶ’
   181вҶ’**Use when:**
   182вҶ’- Starting a new task
   183вҶ’- Unsure of the workflow
   184вҶ’- Need a quick reference
   185вҶ’
   186вҶ’---
   187вҶ’
   188вҶ’## Setup Instructions
   189вҶ’
   190вҶ’### Initial Setup (One-Time)
   191вҶ’
   192вҶ’The automation tools are already set up in this repository. No additional installation required.
   193вҶ’
   194вҶ’**Verify setup:**
   195вҶ’```bash
   196вҶ’# Check git hook exists and is executable
   197вҶ’ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   198вҶ’
   199вҶ’# Check automation files exist
   200вҶ’ls -la .claude/
   201вҶ’
   202вҶ’# Expected output:
   203вҶ’# .claude/
   204вҶ’# в”ңв”Җв”Җ automation-guide.md         (this file)
   205вҶ’# в”ңв”Җв”Җ workflow-checklist.md       (step-by-step checklist)
   206вҶ’# в”ңв”Җв”Җ templates/
   207вҶ’# в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ task-template.md        (task file template)
   208вҶ’# в””в”Җв”Җ commands/
   209вҶ’#     в””в”Җв”Җ start-task.md           (custom command)
   210вҶ’```
   211вҶ’
   212вҶ’### For New Developers
   213вҶ’
   214вҶ’1. **Clone repository**
   215вҶ’2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-11)
   216вҶ’3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
   217вҶ’4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   218вҶ’5. **Test git hook:**
   219вҶ’   ```bash
   220вҶ’   # Make a small change to src/ file
   221вҶ’   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx
   222вҶ’
   223вҶ’   # Try to commit without staging task file
   224вҶ’   git add src/main.tsx
   225вҶ’   git commit -m "test"
   226вҶ’   # Should be BLOCKED
   227вҶ’
   228вҶ’   # Stage task file and retry
   229вҶ’   git add tasks/some-task.md
   230вҶ’   git commit -m "test"
   231вҶ’   # Should succeed
   232вҶ’
   233вҶ’   # Revert test
   234вҶ’   git reset HEAD~1
   235вҶ’   git checkout src/main.tsx
   236вҶ’   ```
   237вҶ’
   238вҶ’---
   239вҶ’
   240вҶ’## How It Works
   241вҶ’
   242вҶ’### Git Hook Workflow

   319вҶ’## Troubleshooting
   320вҶ’
   321вҶ’### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running
   322вҶ’
   323вҶ’**Symptoms:**
   324вҶ’- Can commit code without task file
   325вҶ’- No error message appears
   326вҶ’
   327вҶ’**Causes & Fixes:**
   328вҶ’
   329вҶ’**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
   330вҶ’```bash
   331вҶ’# Fix: Make it executable
   332вҶ’chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   333вҶ’```
   334вҶ’
   335вҶ’**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
   336вҶ’```bash
   337вҶ’# Check if file exists
   338вҶ’ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   339вҶ’
   340вҶ’# If missing, recreate from template (if you have one)
   341вҶ’# Or re-run setup script
   342вҶ’```
   343вҶ’
   344вҶ’**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
   345вҶ’```bash
   346вҶ’# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
   347вҶ’git commit  # вң… Correct
   348вҶ’git commit --no-verify  # вқҢ Bypasses hook
   349вҶ’```
   350вҶ’
   351вҶ’**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
   352вҶ’```bash
   353вҶ’# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe)
   354вҶ’# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
   355вҶ’```
   356вҶ’
   357вҶ’---
   358вҶ’
   359вҶ’### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits
   360вҶ’
   361вҶ’**Symptoms:**
   362вҶ’- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
   363вҶ’- False positive error
   364вҶ’
   365вҶ’**Causes & Fixes:**
   366вҶ’
   367вҶ’**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
   368вҶ’```bash
   369вҶ’# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
   370вҶ’git add tasks/my-feature.md  # вң… Correct
   371вҶ’git add my-feature.md         # вқҢ Wrong location
   372вҶ’git add docs/task.md          # вқҢ Wrong location
   373вҶ’```
   374вҶ’
   375вҶ’**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
   376вҶ’```bash
   377вҶ’# Check what's staged
   378вҶ’git diff --cached --name-only
   379вҶ’
   380вҶ’# Ensure task file is in the list
   381вҶ’# If not, stage it:
   382вҶ’git add tasks/my-feature.md
   383вҶ’```
   384вҶ’
   385вҶ’**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
   386вҶ’```bash
   387вҶ’# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
   388вҶ’git add tasks/my-feature.md   # вң… Correct
   389вҶ’git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # вқҢ Wrong extension
   390вҶ’```
   391вҶ’

   396вҶ’**Symptoms:**
   397вҶ’- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
   398вҶ’- File not found error
   399вҶ’
   400вҶ’**Fixes:**
   401вҶ’```bash
   402вҶ’# Check if template exists
   403вҶ’ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md
   404вҶ’
   405вҶ’# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
   406вҶ’ls -la .claude/
   407вҶ’
   408вҶ’# Ensure you're in repository root

   413вҶ’
   414вҶ’### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File
   415вҶ’
   416вҶ’**Symptoms:**
   417вҶ’- Code commits go through without task file
   418вҶ’- Hook seems to not detect code changes
   419вҶ’
   420вҶ’**Causes & Fixes:**
   421вҶ’
   422вҶ’**Cause 1:** Code not in src/ directory
   423вҶ’```bash
   424вҶ’# Hook only checks src/ directory
   425вҶ’# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it
   426вҶ’
   427вҶ’# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
   428вҶ’# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
   429вҶ’# Find line: CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/')
   430вҶ’# Add more patterns: grep -E '^(src/|lib/|components/)'
   431вҶ’```
   432вҶ’
   433вҶ’**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
   434вҶ’```bash
   435вҶ’# Hook ignores .md files in src/
   436вҶ’# (to allow README updates without task file)
   437вҶ’
   438вҶ’# This is intentional behavior
   439вҶ’```
   440вҶ’

   443вҶ’### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying
   444вҶ’
   445вҶ’**Symptoms:**
   446вҶ’- Commit blocked but no error message shown
   447вҶ’- Silent failure
   448вҶ’
   449вҶ’**Fixes:**
   450вҶ’```bash
   451вҶ’# Test hook manually
   452вҶ’bash .git/hooks/pre-commit
   453вҶ’
   454вҶ’# Check for syntax errors
   455вҶ’bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit
   456вҶ’
   457вҶ’# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
   458вҶ’# Some terminals don't support color codes
   459вҶ’```
   460вҶ’
   461вҶ’---
   462вҶ’
   463вҶ’## Customization
   464вҶ’
   465вҶ’### Adding More File Patterns to Hook
   466вҶ’
   467вҶ’**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)
   468вҶ’
   469вҶ’```bash
   470вҶ’# Current pattern (only src/)
   471вҶ’CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')
   472вҶ’
   473вҶ’# Add more directories (lib/, config/, etc.)
   474вҶ’CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
   475вҶ’```
   476вҶ’
   477вҶ’### Excluding Specific Files from Hook
   478вҶ’
   479вҶ’**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)
   480вҶ’
   481вҶ’```bash
   482вҶ’# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
   483вҶ’CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$)')
   484вҶ’```
   485вҶ’
   486вҶ’### Customizing Task Template
   487вҶ’
   488вҶ’**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   489вҶ’
   490вҶ’Add/remove sections as needed:
   491вҶ’- Additional checklists
   492вҶ’- Project-specific requirements
   493вҶ’- Custom metadata fields
   494вҶ’
   495вҶ’**Example additions:**
   496вҶ’```markdown
   497вҶ’## Security Checklist
   498вҶ’- [ ] Input validation added
   499вҶ’- [ ] XSS prevention verified
   500вҶ’- [ ] SQL injection prevented
   501вҶ’
   502вҶ’## Performance Checklist
   503вҶ’- [ ] Bundle size impact < 10KB
   504вҶ’- [ ] No memory leaks
   505вҶ’- [ ] Animations 60fps
   506вҶ’```
   507вҶ’
   508вҶ’### Adding New Custom Commands
   509вҶ’
   510вҶ’**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`
   511вҶ’
   512вҶ’Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`
   513вҶ’
   514вҶ’```markdown
   515вҶ’# Review Task Checklist
   516вҶ’
   517вҶ’Before marking task complete:
   518вҶ’
   519вҶ’- [ ] Review section filled out
   520вҶ’- [ ] All files documented
   521вҶ’- [ ] Testing complete
   522вҶ’- [ ] Documentation updated
   523вҶ’- [ ] Bundle size checked
   524вҶ’```
   525вҶ’
   526вҶ’### Adjusting Hook Error Messages
   527вҶ’
   528вҶ’**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Lines 30-70)
   529вҶ’
   530вҶ’Customize:
   531вҶ’- Error message text
   532вҶ’- Color scheme (ANSI codes)
   533вҶ’- Instructions provided
   534вҶ’- Examples shown
   535вҶ’
   536вҶ’---
   537вҶ’
   538вҶ’## Best Practices
   539вҶ’
   540вҶ’### 1. Always Start with the Checklist
   541вҶ’
   542вҶ’Before starting any task:
   543вҶ’```bash
   544вҶ’# Read the workflow checklist
   545вҶ’cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   546вҶ’
   547вҶ’# Or use custom command
   548вҶ’/start-task  # If available in Claude Code
   549вҶ’```
   550вҶ’
   551вҶ’### 2. Create Task File FIRST
   552вҶ’
   553вҶ’```bash
   554вҶ’# вң… CORRECT ORDER
   555вҶ’cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
   556вҶ’# Fill out the task file
   557вҶ’# THEN start coding
   558вҶ’
   559вҶ’# вқҢ WRONG ORDER
   560вҶ’# Start coding first
   561вҶ’# Create task file later (will forget details!)
   562вҶ’```
   563вҶ’
   564вҶ’### 3. Update Task File as You Work
   565вҶ’
   566вҶ’```markdown
   567вҶ’## Todo Checklist
   568вҶ’
   569вҶ’### Phase 1
   570вҶ’- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE (took 30 min)
   571вҶ’- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
   572вҶ’- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for user input)
   573вҶ’```
   574вҶ’
   575вҶ’Keep the task file up to date, don't batch updates at the end.
   576вҶ’
   577вҶ’### 4. Fill Out Review Section Immediately
   578вҶ’
   579вҶ’When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
   580вҶ’- Summary of changes
   581вҶ’- Files created/modified
   582вҶ’- Testing status
   583вҶ’- Issues encountered
   584вҶ’
   585вҶ’Don't wait days/weeks - you'll forget important details!
   586вҶ’
   587вҶ’### 5. Stage Task File with Code
   588вҶ’
   589вҶ’```bash
   590вҶ’# вң… CORRECT - Stage both together
   591вҶ’git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   592вҶ’git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
   593вҶ’git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"
   594вҶ’
   595вҶ’# вқҢ WRONG - Separate commits
   596вҶ’git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   597вҶ’git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
   598вҶ’# Oops, forgot task file! (Hook will block this)
   599вҶ’```
   600вҶ’
   601вҶ’### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names
   602вҶ’
   603вҶ’```bash
   604вҶ’# вң… GOOD - Descriptive, specific
   605вҶ’tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
   606вҶ’tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
   607вҶ’tasks/refactor-zustand-stores.md
   608вҶ’
   609вҶ’# вқҢ BAD - Vague, generic
   610вҶ’tasks/todo.md
   611вҶ’tasks/task1.md
   612вҶ’tasks/stuff.md
   613вҶ’tasks/changes.md
   614вҶ’```
   615вҶ’
   616вҶ’### 7. One Task File Per Feature
   617вҶ’
   618вҶ’Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.
   619вҶ’
   620вҶ’```bash
   621вҶ’# вң… CORRECT - Separate files
   622вҶ’tasks/add-search-feature.md
   623вҶ’tasks/add-export-feature.md
   624вҶ’
   625вҶ’# вқҢ WRONG - One giant file
   626вҶ’tasks/all-features.md
   627вҶ’```
   628вҶ’
   629вҶ’### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES
   630вҶ’
   631вҶ’```bash
   632вҶ’# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
   633вҶ’git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
   634вҶ’git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
   635вҶ’git commit --no-verify  # Doc-only changes (maybe)
   636вҶ’
   637вҶ’# NOT for:
   638вҶ’git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
   639вҶ’git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later" (you won't!)
   640вҶ’```
   641вҶ’
   642вҶ’### 9. Keep Documentation Updated
   643вҶ’
   644вҶ’If architecture changes, update docs:
   645вҶ’```bash
   646вҶ’git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
   647вҶ’git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
   648вҶ’git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md  # New architecture doc
   649вҶ’git commit -m "feat: Add undo system
   650вҶ’
   651вҶ’Closes #42"
   652вҶ’```
   653вҶ’
   654вҶ’### 10. Reference CLAUDE.md Rules
   655вҶ’
   656вҶ’When in doubt, check CLAUDE.md:
   657вҶ’```bash
   658вҶ’# Quick reference
   659вҶ’cat CLAUDE.md | grep "^[0-9])"
   660вҶ’
   661вҶ’# Full read
   662вҶ’cat CLAUDE.md
   663вҶ’```
   664вҶ’
   665вҶ’---
   666вҶ’
   667вҶ’## Integration with Claude Code
   668вҶ’
   669вҶ’### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code
   670вҶ’
   671вҶ’1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
   672вҶ’2. **Use task template** for all planning
   673вҶ’3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
   674вҶ’4. **Fill review section** when done
   675вҶ’5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged
   676вҶ’
   677вҶ’### Behavioral Changes for Claude Code
   678вҶ’
   679вҶ’**Before starting ANY task:**
   680вҶ’- вң… Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   681вҶ’- вң… Create task file from template
   682вҶ’- вң… Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)
   683вҶ’
   684вҶ’**During implementation:**
   685вҶ’- вң… Update todos as you complete them
   686вҶ’- вң… Document decisions in task file
   687вҶ’- вң… Track time estimates vs actual
   688вҶ’
   689вҶ’**When complete:**
   690вҶ’- вң… Fill out review section
   691вҶ’- вң… Update docs/ if architecture changed
   692вҶ’- вң… Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)
   693вҶ’
   694вҶ’**On every commit:**
   695вҶ’- вң… Stage code files AND task file together
   696вҶ’- вң… Git hook verifies compliance
   697вҶ’- вң… Commit proceeds
   698вҶ’
   699вҶ’---
   700вҶ’
   701вҶ’## Appendix
   702вҶ’
   703вҶ’### File Structure Reference
   704вҶ’
   705вҶ’```
   706вҶ’system.knowledgebase/
   707вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ .git/
   708вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ hooks/
   709вҶ’в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
   710вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ .claude/
   711вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ automation-guide.md         # This file
   712вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ workflow-checklist.md       # Step-by-step checklist
   713вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ templates/
   714вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ task-template.md        # Task file template
   715вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ commands/
   716вҶ’в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ start-task.md           # Custom command
   717вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ tasks/
   718вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ [feature-name].md           # Task files (one per feature)
   719вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ CLAUDE.md                        # Main workflow guide (rules #1-11)
   720вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ README.md                        # User-facing documentation
   721вҶ’```
   722вҶ’
   723вҶ’### Git Hook Exit Codes
   724вҶ’
   725вҶ’- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
   726вҶ’- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)
   727вҶ’
   728вҶ’### ANSI Color Codes Used
   729вҶ’
   730вҶ’```bash
   731вҶ’RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
   732вҶ’YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
   733вҶ’GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
   734вҶ’BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
   735вҶ’NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
   736вҶ’```
   737вҶ’
   738вҶ’---
   739вҶ’
   740вҶ’## Support & Questions
   741вҶ’
   742вҶ’**For questions about:**
   743вҶ’- **CLAUDE.md workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root
   744вҶ’- **Workflow steps:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   745вҶ’- **Task templates:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   746вҶ’- **Git hook issues:** See troubleshooting section above
   747вҶ’- **Feature requests:** Create GitHub issue
   748вҶ’
   749вҶ’---
   750вҶ’
   751вҶ’**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
   752вҶ’**Maintained By:** Engineering Team
   753вҶ’**Version:** 1.0
   754вҶ’
   755вҶ’**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. They catch mistakes BEFORE they become problems. Follow the workflow, and your development process will be smoother and more consistent.
   756вҶ’

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide

**Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Customization](#customization)
8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)

---

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md rules #1-11 by:

вң… **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
вң… **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
вң… **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
вң… **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

---

## Quick Start

### For Developers

1. **Before starting any task:**
   ```bash
   # Check workflow checklist
   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   ```

2. **Create task file from template:**
   ```bash
   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   ```

3. **Git hook automatically enforces:**
   - Task file must be staged when committing code changes
   - Will block commit if missing

### For Claude Code

1. **Use `/start-task` command** (if available)
2. **Reference workflow checklist** before starting
3. **Use task template** for all new tasks
4. **Git hook will catch** if task file missing

---

## Automation Tools

### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook

**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7

**How it works:**
1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)

**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
```
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
  вқҢ COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.

Changed code files:
  - src/components/Modal.tsx
  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts

Required Action:
  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
  3. Retry commit: git commit
```

**Emergency Bypass:**
```bash
git commit --no-verify
```
вҡ пёҸ **Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies

---

### 2. Workflow Checklist

**Location:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
**Enforces:** All rules #1-11

**When to use:**
- Before starting ANY task
- During implementation (reference as needed)
- Before marking work complete
- Before creating git commits

**Sections:**
- вң… Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
- вң… During Implementation
- вң… When Complete
- вң… Git Commit Checklist
- вң… Rules #1-11 Quick Reference

**View checklist:**
```bash
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
```

---

### 3. Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7

**Sections included:**
- Overview and problem statement
- Files to read
- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
- Todo checklist with time estimates
- Design considerations
- Implementation notes
- Testing checklist
- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)

**Create new task:**
```bash
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Good naming examples:**
- вң… `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- вң… `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- вң… `tasks/notes-ux-improvements.md`

**Bad naming examples:**
- вқҢ `tasks/todo.md`
- вқҢ `tasks/task1.md`
- вқҢ `tasks/new.md`

---

### 4. Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow reminders

#### `/start-task` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
**Purpose:** Comprehensive reminder before starting any task

**What it covers:**
1. Sync with main branch (Rule #11)
2. Read CLAUDE.md (Rules #1-11)
3. Create task file from template
4. Fill out task plan
5. Get user approval (Rule #3)
6. Implementation workflow
7. Review section and documentation updates

**Use when:**
- Starting a new task
- Unsure of the workflow
- Need a quick reference

---

## Setup Instructions

### Initial Setup (One-Time)

The automation tools are already set up in this repository. No additional installation required.

**Verify setup:**
```bash
# Check git hook exists and is executable
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check automation files exist
ls -la .claude/

# Expected output:
# .claude/
# в”ңв”Җв”Җ automation-guide.md         (this file)
# в”ңв”Җв”Җ workflow-checklist.md       (step-by-step checklist)
# в”ңв”Җв”Җ templates/
# в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ task-template.md        (task file template)
# в””в”Җв”Җ commands/
#     в””в”Җв”Җ start-task.md           (custom command)
```

### For New Developers

1. **Clone repository**
2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-11)
3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
5. **Test git hook:**
   ```bash
   # Make a small change to src/ file
   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx

   # Try to commit without staging task file
   git add src/main.tsx
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should be BLOCKED

   # Stage task file and retry
   git add tasks/some-task.md
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should succeed

   # Revert test
   git reset HEAD~1
   git checkout src/main.tsx
   ```

---

## How It Works

### Git Hook Workflow

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Developer runs: git commit                                  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                     в”Ӯ
                     в–ј
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

## Troubleshooting

### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running

**Symptoms:**
- Can commit code without task file
- No error message appears

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
```bash
# Fix: Make it executable
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
```bash
# Check if file exists
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# If missing, recreate from template (if you have one)
# Or re-run setup script
```

**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
```bash
# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
git commit  # вң… Correct
git commit --no-verify  # вқҢ Bypasses hook
```

**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
```bash
# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe)
# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
```

---

### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits

**Symptoms:**
- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
- False positive error

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
```bash
# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
git add tasks/my-feature.md  # вң… Correct
git add my-feature.md         # вқҢ Wrong location
git add docs/task.md          # вқҢ Wrong location
```

**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
```bash
# Check what's staged
git diff --cached --name-only

# Ensure task file is in the list
# If not, stage it:
git add tasks/my-feature.md
```

**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
```bash
# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
git add tasks/my-feature.md   # вң… Correct
git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # вқҢ Wrong extension
```

---

### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template

**Symptoms:**
- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
- File not found error

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Check if template exists
ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md

# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
ls -la .claude/

# Ensure you're in repository root
pwd  # Should end with system.knowledgebase
```

---

### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File

**Symptoms:**
- Code commits go through without task file
- Hook seems to not detect code changes

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Code not in src/ directory
```bash
# Hook only checks src/ directory
# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it

# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
# Find line: CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/')
# Add more patterns: grep -E '^(src/|lib/|components/)'
```

**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
```bash
# Hook ignores .md files in src/
# (to allow README updates without task file)

# This is intentional behavior
```

---

### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying

**Symptoms:**
- Commit blocked but no error message shown
- Silent failure

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Test hook manually
bash .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check for syntax errors
bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
# Some terminals don't support color codes
```

---

## Customization

### Adding More File Patterns to Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)

```bash
# Current pattern (only src/)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')

# Add more directories (lib/, config/, etc.)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
```

### Excluding Specific Files from Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)

```bash
# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$)')
```

### Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

Add/remove sections as needed:
- Additional checklists
- Project-specific requirements
- Custom metadata fields

**Example additions:**
```markdown
## Security Checklist
- [ ] Input validation added
- [ ] XSS prevention verified
- [ ] SQL injection prevented

## Performance Checklist
- [ ] Bundle size impact < 10KB
- [ ] No memory leaks
- [ ] Animations 60fps
```

### Adding New Custom Commands

**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`

Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`

```markdown
# Review Task Checklist

Before marking task complete:

- [ ] Review section filled out
- [ ] All files documented
- [ ] Testing complete
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Bundle size checked
```

### Adjusting Hook Error Messages

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Lines 30-70)

Customize:
- Error message text
- Color scheme (ANSI codes)
- Instructions provided
- Examples shown

## Best Practices

### 1. Always Start with the Checklist

Before starting any task:
```bash
# Read the workflow checklist
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md

# Or use custom command
/start-task  # If available in Claude Code
```

### 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# вң… CORRECT ORDER
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out the task file
# THEN start coding

# вқҢ WRONG ORDER
# Start coding first
# Create task file later (will forget details!)
```

### 3. Update Task File as You Work

```markdown
## Todo Checklist

### Phase 1
- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE (took 30 min)
- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for user input)
```

Keep the task file up to date, don't batch updates at the end.

### 4. Fill Out Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified
- Testing status
- Issues encountered

Don't wait days/weeks - you'll forget important details!

### 5. Stage Task File with Code

```bash
# вң… CORRECT - Stage both together
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"

# вқҢ WRONG - Separate commits
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
# Oops, forgot task file! (Hook will block this)
```

### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names

```bash
# вң… GOOD - Descriptive, specific
tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
tasks/refactor-zustand-stores.md

# вқҢ BAD - Vague, generic
tasks/todo.md
tasks/task1.md
tasks/stuff.md
tasks/changes.md
```

### 7. One Task File Per Feature

Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.

```bash
# вң… CORRECT - Separate files
tasks/add-search-feature.md
tasks/add-export-feature.md

# вқҢ WRONG - One giant file
tasks/all-features.md
```

### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES

```bash
# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
git commit --no-verify  # Doc-only changes (maybe)

# NOT for:
git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later" (you won't!)
```

### 9. Keep Documentation Updated

If architecture changes, update docs:
```bash
git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md  # New architecture doc
git commit -m "feat: Add undo system

Closes #42"
```

### 10. Reference CLAUDE.md Rules

When in doubt, check CLAUDE.md:
```bash
# Quick reference
cat CLAUDE.md | grep "^[0-9])"

# Full read
cat CLAUDE.md
```

## Integration with Claude Code

### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code

1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
2. **Use task template** for all planning
3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
4. **Fill review section** when done
5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged

### Behavioral Changes for Claude Code

**Before starting ANY task:**
- вң… Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- вң… Create task file from template
- вң… Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)

**During implementation:**
- вң… Update todos as you complete them
- вң… Document decisions in task file
- вң… Track time estimates vs actual

**When complete:**
- вң… Fill out review section
- вң… Update docs/ if architecture changed
- вң… Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)

**On every commit:**
- вң… Stage code files AND task file together
- вң… Git hook verifies compliance
- вң… Commit proceeds

### Git Hook Exit Codes

- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)

### ANSI Color Codes Used

```bash
RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
```

1вҶ’# Development Principles - Neuman Brain 2вҶ’ 3вҶ’**Last Updated:** November 13, 2025 4вҶ’**Status:** рҹ”’ MANDATORY - All code must follow these principles 5вҶ’**Audience:** Developers, contributors, stakeholders 6вҶ’ 7вҶ’--- 8вҶ’ 9вҶ’## Core Principles (Non-Negotiable) 10вҶ’ 11вҶ’### 1. Privacy First рҹ”’ 12вҶ’ 13вҶ’**Every feature, every line of code, every decision must prioritize user privacy.** 14вҶ’ 15вҶ’#### Mandatory Privacy Requirements: 16вҶ’ 17вҶ’вң… **Local-first by default** 18вҶ’- Data stays on user's device unless they explicitly choose cloud sync 19вҶ’- No telemetry, analytics, or tracking without explicit opt-in 20вҶ’- No data sent to Neuman Brain servers (zero-data-host architecture) 21вҶ’ 22вҶ’вң… **User controls their data** 23вҶ’- Users choose where data is stored (local folder, cloud provider, etc.) 24вҶ’- Easy export at any time (no lock-in) 25вҶ’- Clear communication about where data goes 26вҶ’ 27вҶ’вң… **Encryption everywhere (Q2 2026+)** 28вҶ’- End-to-end encryption for all backups (.brain files) 29вҶ’- User-controlled passphrases (we never see them) 30вҶ’- Zero-knowledge architecture (we can't read user data even if we wanted to) 31вҶ’ 32вҶ’вқҢ **Never allowed:** 33вҶ’- Uploading user data to our servers 34вҶ’- Tracking individual user behavior 35вҶ’- Selling data to third parties 36вҶ’- Building features that require server-side access to content 37вҶ’- Collecting PII without explicit consent 38вҶ’ 39вҶ’#### Privacy Checklist for Every Feature: 40вҶ’ 41вҶ’Before shipping ANY feature, verify: 42вҶ’ 43вҶ’- [ ] Does this feature send data anywhere? (If yes, user must explicitly opt-in) 44вҶ’- [ ] Can this feature work offline? (If not, why not?) 45вҶ’- [ ] Is user data encrypted in transit and at rest? 46вҶ’- [ ] Can users delete/export this data easily? 47вҶ’- [ ] Have we documented privacy implications in user-facing language? 48вҶ’- [ ] Does this respect GDPR/CCPA by default? 49вҶ’ 50вҶ’--- 51вҶ’ 52вҶ’### 2. Security First рҹӣЎпёҸ 53вҶ’ 54вҶ’**Security is not a feature to add later - it's the foundation.** 55вҶ’ 56вҶ’#### Mandatory Security Requirements: 57вҶ’ 58вҶ’вң… **Secure by default** 59вҶ’- All user inputs sanitized (XSS prevention) 60вҶ’- Content Security Policy enforced 61вҶ’- HTTPS only (no mixed content) 62вҶ’- Secure random for crypto operations (crypto.getRandomValues) 63вҶ’ 64вҶ’вң… **Use proven libraries** 65вҶ’- WebCrypto API for encryption (not custom crypto!) 66вҶ’- Established libraries for auth (if ever needed) 67вҶ’- Regular dependency updates (npm audit weekly) 68вҶ’ 69вҶ’вң… **Defense in depth** 70вҶ’- Validate on client AND server (when applicable) 71вҶ’- Principle of least privilege 72вҶ’- Fail securely (errors don't leak sensitive info) 73вҶ’ 74вҶ’вқҢ **Never allowed:** 75вҶ’- Custom crypto implementations 76вҶ’- Storing secrets in code (use env vars, user-controlled passphrases) 77вҶ’- eval() or innerHTML with user content 78вҶ’- Ignoring security warnings 79вҶ’- Skipping security reviews for "speed" 80вҶ’ 81вҶ’#### Security Checklist for Every Feature: 82вҶ’ 83вҶ’Before shipping ANY feature, verify: 84вҶ’ 85вҶ’- [ ] All user inputs are sanitized 86вҶ’- [ ] No XSS vulnerabilities (run automated tests) 87вҶ’- [ ] No SQL injection (if database ever added) 88вҶ’- [ ] Dependencies are up-to-date (npm audit clean) 89вҶ’- [ ] Secrets are not in code or committed to git 90вҶ’- [ ] Error messages don't leak sensitive info 91вҶ’- [ ] Security-sensitive code has been reviewed 92вҶ’ 93вҶ’--- 94вҶ’ 95вҶ’### 3. Beautiful UX First рҹҺЁ 96вҶ’ 97вҶ’**Privacy and security should feel effortless, not burdensome.** 98вҶ’ 99вҶ’#### Mandatory UX Requirements: 100вҶ’ 101вҶ’вң… **Privacy should be invisible** 102вҶ’- Local-first should be the default (no scary consent screens) 103вҶ’- Encryption should happen automatically (users just pick a passphrase) 104вҶ’- No technical jargon ("Your data is encrypted with AES-256-GCM" вқҢ вҶ’ "Only you can read your data" вң…) 105вҶ’ 106вҶ’вң… **Performance matters** 107вҶ’- < 1 second load time 108вҶ’- < 100ms for interactions (typing, clicking) 109вҶ’- Bundle size < 200KB (current: 60KB - maintain this!) 110вҶ’- Works on slow networks 111вҶ’ 112вҶ’вң… **Accessibility is mandatory** 113вҶ’- Keyboard navigation for all features 114вҶ’- ARIA labels for screen readers 115вҶ’- Color contrast meets WCAG AA standards 116вҶ’- Works without JavaScript (progressive enhancement where possible) 117вҶ’ 118вҶ’вң… **Polish everything** 119вҶ’- Animations are smooth (60fps) 120вҶ’- Empty states are helpful, not scary 121вҶ’- Errors are actionable, not technical 122вҶ’- Onboarding is < 30 seconds 123вҶ’ 124вҶ’вқҢ **Never allowed:** 125вҶ’- Sacrificing UX for security (find a better way) 126вҶ’- Laggy interactions (optimize first) 127вҶ’- Confusing privacy settings 128вҶ’- Broken accessibility 129вҶ’- Ugly error messages 130вҶ’ 131вҶ’#### UX Checklist for Every Feature: 132вҶ’ 133вҶ’Before shipping ANY feature, verify: 134вҶ’ 135вҶ’- [ ] Works with keyboard only 136вҶ’- [ ] Screen reader accessible (test with NVDA/VoiceOver) 137вҶ’- [ ] Loads in < 1 second on 3G 138вҶ’- [ ] No layout shift (CLS score < 0.1) 139вҶ’- [ ] Empty states are helpful 140вҶ’- [ ] Error messages are actionable 141вҶ’- [ ] Mobile-friendly (responsive design) 142вҶ’ 143вҶ’--- 144вҶ’ 145вҶ’### 4. Open Source First рҹ“– 146вҶ’ 147вҶ’**Transparency builds trust.** 148вҶ’ 149вҶ’вң… **Everything is MIT licensed** 150вҶ’- All code is public (except user data, obviously) 151вҶ’- No proprietary lock-in 152вҶ’- Community can audit security 153вҶ’ 154вҶ’вң… **Documentation is code** 155вҶ’- Every feature is documented 156вҶ’- Privacy implications explained 157вҶ’- Security decisions justified 158вҶ’ 159вҶ’вң… **Community-driven** 160вҶ’- Accept pull requests 161вҶ’- Responsive to issues 162вҶ’- Credit contributors 163вҶ’ 164вҶ’--- 165вҶ’ 166вҶ’## Development Tools & Testing рҹӣ пёҸ 167вҶ’ 168вҶ’**Enforce quality standards through automation and comprehensive testing.** 169вҶ’ 170вҶ’### Browser Automation & E2E Testing 171вҶ’ 172вҶ’**Playwright MCP** - AI-driven browser testing integrated with Claude Code 173вҶ’ 174вҶ’**Capabilities:** 175вҶ’- **E2E Testing:** Validate all 49 dashboard widgets in real browsers 176вҶ’- **Accessibility Testing:** WCAG AA compliance checks using accessibility tree 177вҶ’- **Cross-Browser Validation:** Chrome, Firefox, Safari rendering consistency 178вҶ’- **Visual Regression:** Screenshot comparison before/after changes 179вҶ’- **Test Generation:** Claude writes Playwright tests from natural language 180вҶ’ 181вҶ’**Usage:** 182вҶ’- Configured in `.mcp.json` (Playwright MCP server) 183вҶ’- Ask Claude: "Test the Calendar widget drag-and-drop functionality" 184вҶ’- Ask Claude: "Check all widgets for WCAG AA violations" 185вҶ’- Ask Claude: "Write E2E tests for the Notes editor slash commands" 186вҶ’ 187вҶ’**Integration:** 188вҶ’- Tests go in `src/__tests__/e2e/` directory 189вҶ’- Run alongside Vitest unit tests: `npm run test` 190вҶ’- Use in CI/CD for pre-deployment validation 191вҶ’ 192вҶ’**Why it matters:** Playwright MCP makes accessibility testing enforceable (not aspirational) and catches bugs before users encounter them. 193вҶ’ 194вҶ’**See:** CLAUDE.md В§ Tool Usage Guide вҶ’ MCP-Specific Tools for detailed patterns. 195вҶ’ 196вҶ’### Unit Testing 197вҶ’ 198вҶ’**Vitest + React Testing Library** - # Development Principles - Neuman Brain **Last Updated:** November 13, 2025 **Status:** рҹ”’ MANDATORY - All code must follow these principles **Audience:** Developers, contributors, stakeholders --- ## Core Principles (Non-Negotiable) ### 1. Privacy First рҹ”’ **Every feature, every line of code, every decision must prioritize user privacy.** #### Mandatory Privacy Requirements: вң… **Local-first by default** - Data stays on user's device unless they explicitly choose cloud sync - No telemetry, analytics, or tracking without explicit opt-in - No data sent to Neuman Brain servers (zero-data-host architecture) вң… **User controls their data** - Users choose where data is stored (local folder, cloud provider, etc.) - Easy export at any time (no lock-in) - Clear communication about where data goes вң… **Encryption everywhere (Q2 2026+)** - End-to-end encryption for all backups (.brain files) - User-controlled passphrases (we never see them) - Zero-knowledge architecture (we can't read user data even if we wanted to) вқҢ **Never allowed:** - Uploading user data to our servers - Tracking individual user behavior - Selling data to third parties - Building features that require server-side access to content - Collecting PII without explicit consent #### Privacy Checklist for Every Feature: Before shipping ANY feature, verify: - [ ] Does this feature send data anywhere? (If yes, user must explicitly opt-in) - [ ] Can this feature work offline? (If not, why not?) - [ ] Is user data encrypted in transit and at rest? - [ ] Can users delete/export this data easily? - [ ] Have we documented privacy implications in user-facing language? - [ ] Does this respect GDPR/CCPA by default? --- ### 2. Security First рҹӣЎпёҸ **Security is not a feature to add later - it's the foundation.** #### Mandatory Security Requirements: вң… **Secure by default** - All user inputs sanitized (XSS prevention) - Content Security Policy enforced - HTTPS only (no mixed content) - Secure random for crypto operations (crypto.getRandomValues) вң… **Use proven libraries** - WebCrypto API for encryption (not custom crypto!) - Established libraries for auth (if ever needed) - Regular dependency updates (npm audit weekly) вң… **Defense in depth** - Validate on client AND server (when applicable) - Principle of least privilege - Fail securely (errors don't leak sensitive info) вқҢ **Never allowed:** - Custom crypto implementations - Storing secrets in code (use env vars, user-controlled passphrases) - eval() or innerHTML with user content - Ignoring security warnings - Skipping security reviews for "speed" #### Security Checklist for Every Feature: Before shipping ANY feature, verify: - [ ] All user inputs are sanitized - [ ] No XSS vulnerabilities (run automated tests) - [ ] No SQL injection (if database ever added) - [ ] Dependencies are up-to-date (npm audit clean) - [ ] Secrets are not in code or committed to git - [ ] Error messages don't leak sensitive info - [ ] Security-sensitive code has been reviewed --- ### 3. Beautiful UX First рҹҺЁ **Privacy and security should feel effortless, not burdensome.** #### Mandatory UX Requirements: вң… **Privacy should be invisible** - Local-first should be the default (no scary consent screens) - Encryption should happen automatically (users just pick a passphrase) - No technical jargon ("Your data is encrypted with AES-256-GCM" вқҢ вҶ’ "Only you can read your data" вң…) вң… **Performance matters** - < 1 second load time - < 100ms for interactions (typing, clicking) - Bundle size < 200KB (current: 60KB - maintain this!) - Works on slow networks вң… **Accessibility is mandatory** - Keyboard navigation for all features - ARIA labels for screen readers - Color contrast meets WCAG AA standards - Works without JavaScript (progressive enhancement where possible) вң… **Polish everything** - Animations are smooth (60fps) - Empty states are helpful, not scary - Errors are actionable, not technical - Onboarding is < 30 seconds вқҢ **Never allowed:** - Sacrificing UX for security (find a better way) - Laggy interactions (optimize first) - Confusing privacy settings - Broken accessibility - Ugly error messages #### UX Checklist for Every Feature: Before shipping ANY feature, verify: - [ ] Works with keyboard only - [ ] Screen reader accessible (test with NVDA/VoiceOver) - [ ] Loads in < 1 second on 3G - [ ] No layout shift (CLS score < 0.1) - [ ] Empty states are helpful - [ ] Error messages are actionable - [ ] Mobile-friendly (responsive design) --- ### 4. Open Source First рҹ“– **Transparency builds trust.** вң… **Everything is MIT licensed** - All code is public (except user data, obviously) - No proprietary lock-in - Community can audit security вң… **Documentation is code** - Every feature is documented - Privacy implications explained - Security decisions justified вң… **Community-driven** - Accept pull requests - Responsive to issues - Credit contributors --- ## Development Tools & Testing рҹӣ пёҸ **Enforce quality standards through automation and comprehensive testing.** ### Browser Automation & E2E Testing **Playwright MCP** - AI-driven browser testing integrated with Claude Code **Capabilities:** - **E2E Testing:** Validate all 49 dashboard widgets in real browsers - **Accessibility Testing:** WCAG AA compliance checks using accessibility tree - **Cross-Browser Validation:** Chrome, Firefox, Safari rendering consistency - **Visual Regression:** Screenshot comparison before/after changes - **Test Generation:** Claude writes Playwright tests from natural language **Usage:** - Configured in `.mcp.json` (Playwright MCP server) - Ask Claude: "Test the Calendar widget drag-and-drop functionality" - Ask Claude: "Check all widgets for WCAG AA violations" - Ask Claude: "Write E2E tests for the Notes editor slash commands" **Integration:** - Tests go in `src/__tests__/e2e/` directory - Run alongside Vitest unit tests: `npm run test` - Use in CI/CD for pre-deployment validation **Why it matters:** Playwright MCP makes accessibility testing enforceable (not aspirational) and catches bugs before users encounter them. **See:** CLAUDE.md В§ Tool Usage Guide вҶ’ MCP-Specific Tools for detailed patterns. ### Unit Testing **Vitest + React Testing Library** - Fast, isolated component testing **See:** `.claude/skills/test-specialist/` for comprehensive Vitest + RTL patterns. --- ## Feature Gating for Monetization рҹ’° **Privacy and beautiful UX are FREE forever. Premium features can be gated.** ### Free Tier (Always Free) вң… **Core productivity features:** - Unlimited notes (once Notes MVP ships) - Unlimited tasks (Kanban) - Unlimited calendar events - Unlimited time tracking - Unlimited habits - Local storage (unlimited via IndexedDB) - Manual export/import (.brain files) - File System Access API auto-save - All privacy features (encryption, local-first) - Beautiful UX (no ads, no nags) вң… **Privacy features (always free):** - End-to-end encryption - Local-first storage - Zero telemetry - User-controlled backups **Philosophy:** Core "Second Brain" functionality should be free forever. We compete on privacy and UX, not paywalls. --- ### Pro Tier ($7-9/month or $79-99/year) **Advanced productivity features that require development effort:** рҹ”’ **Advanced Notes Features** (Q2-Q3 2026): - Graph view (visual note connections) - Advanced search (regex, filters, saved searches) - Templates library (50+ templates vs 3 basic) - Custom CSS/themes - Advanced dataview queries (Notion-like databases) рҹ”’ **Collaboration Features** (Q2-Q3 2026): - Real-time collaboration (2+ users) - Share notes/tasks with permissions - Team workspaces (up to 10 people) - Comments and @mentions - Activity feed рҹ”’ **Integrations** (Q3-Q4 2026): - Calendar sync (Google Calendar, Outlook) - Task sync (Todoist, Asana, etc.) - Zapier/Make automation - API access рҹ”’ **Advanced Time Tracking** (Q2 2026): - Billable hours tracking - Invoice generation - Advanced reports (custom date ranges, exports) - Time tracking integrations (Harvest, Toggl) рҹ”’ **AI Features** (2027+) - ONLY if user brings own API key: - AI note suggestions - Smart tagging - Auto-summarization - (Privacy: AI runs on user's API key, we never see content) рҹ”’ **Priority Support**: - Email support (< 24hr response) - Bug priority escalation - Feature request priority --- ### Enterprise Tier ($49-99/user/month - 2027) **Team features and compliance:** рҹ”’ **Team Management**: - Unlimited team members - Advanced permissions (custom roles) - Team analytics (aggregated, not individual) - SSO (SAML, OAuth) рҹ”’ **Compliance & Security**: - Audit logs - Data retention policies - SOC 2 compliance (future) - HIPAA compliance (future) рҹ”’ **Self-Hosted Option**: - Self-hosted sync server (Docker image) - On-premise deployment - Custom domain рҹ”’ **Dedicated Support**: - Slack/Discord channel - Video onboarding - Custom training --- ## How Feature Gating Works Technically ### Client-Side Feature Flags ```typescript // src/services/subscriptions.ts export type SubscriptionTier = 'free' | 'pro' | 'enterprise'; export interface UserSubscription { tier: SubscriptionTier; expiresAt?: Date; features: string[]; } // Feature flags export const FEATURES = { // Always free NOTES_BASIC: 'notes_basic', TASKS: 'tasks', CALENDAR: 'calendar', TIME_TRACKING_BASIC: 'time_tracking_basic', HABITS: 'habits', ENCRYPTION: 'encryption', // Pro tier GRAPH_VIEW: 'graph_view', ADVANCED_SEARCH: 'advanced_search', REAL_TIME_COLLAB: 'real_time_collab', INTEGRATIONS: 'integrations', BILLABLE_HOURS: 'billable_hours', // Enterprise tier TEAM_MANAGEMENT: 'team_management', AUDIT_LOGS: 'audit_logs', SSO: 'sso', SELF_HOSTED: 'self_hosted', } as const; // Check if user has access to feature export function hasFeature(feature: string): boolean { const subscription = getUserSubscription(); // Always allow free features const freeFeatures = [ FEATURES.NOTES_BASIC, FEATURES.TASKS, FEATURES.CALENDAR, FEATURES.TIME_TRACKING_BASIC, FEATURES.HABITS, FEATURES.ENCRYPTION, ]; if (freeFeatures.includes(feature)) { return true; } // Check tier if (subscription.tier === 'free') { return false; } if (subscription.tier === 'pro' && PRO_FEATURES.includes(feature)) { return true; } if (subscription.tier === 'enterprise') { return true; // Enterprise gets everything } return false; } // Get user subscription (from local storage or server) function getUserSubscription(): UserSubscription { // For now, everyone is free tier // Later: check localStorage for license key or call server const tier = localStorage.getItem('subscription_tier') || 'free'; return { tier: tier as SubscriptionTier, features: [], }; } ``` ### Usage in Components ```typescript // src/components/GraphView.tsx import { hasFeature, FEATURES } from '../services/subscriptions'; export function GraphView() { const hasAccess = hasFeature(FEATURES.GRAPH_VIEW); if (!hasAccess) { return (

рҹ“Ҡ Graph View (Pro)

Visualize connections between your notes.

); } // Render actual graph view return ; } ``` ### License Validation (Privacy-Respecting) **Option 1: Local License Keys (Recommended)** ```typescript // User buys Pro вҶ’ Gets license key: NBRAIN-PRO-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX // Stores in localStorage // App validates locally (offline-first) export function validateLicense(key: string): SubscriptionTier { // Simple format validation if (key.startsWith('NBRAIN-PRO-')) { return 'pro'; } if (key.startsWith('NBRAIN-ENT-')) { return 'enterprise'; } return 'free'; } ``` **Option 2: Server Validation (For Collaboration Features)** ```typescript // Only for features that REQUIRE server (real-time collab) // Validates license against server // Still works offline for local features export async function validateLicenseWithServer(key: string): Promise { try { const response = await fetch('https://brain.neuman.dev/api/validate', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ key }), }); return response.ok; } catch { // Offline: allow if key format is valid return validateLicense(key) !== 'free'; } } ``` --- ## Implementation Order for Monetization ### Phase 1: Free Tier (Q1-Q2 2026) - Build all core features as free - Focus on privacy, security, beautiful UX - No paywalls yet - Validate product-market fit ### Phase 2: Pro Tier (Q4 2026) - Add advanced features (graph view, advanced search) - Implement license key system (local validation) - Add upgrade prompts (non-intrusive) - Launch Pro tier ($7-9/month) ### Phase 3: Collaboration (Q1 2027) - Build real-time collaboration (requires server) - Server validates licenses for collab features - Add team workspaces ### Phase 4: Enterprise (Q2-Q3 2027) - Add team management, audit logs - Self-hosted option - SOC 2 compliance - Launch Enterprise tier --- ## Privacy-First Monetization Rules вң… **Allowed:** - Gating advanced features (graph view, integrations, etc.) - Gating collaboration features (requires server) - Gating premium support - Gating enterprise features (SSO, audit logs) вқҢ **NEVER allowed:** - Gating core productivity features (notes, tasks, calendar) - Gating privacy features (encryption, local-first) - Limiting storage (IndexedDB is unlimited on free tier) - Ads or selling user data - Tracking to upsell (no "you used X feature 10 times, upgrade!") - Annoying upgrade prompts (one subtle banner max) --- ## Feature Matrix (Free vs Pro vs Enterprise) | Feature | Free | Pro | Enterprise | |---------|------|-----|------------| | **Core Features** | | Notes (unlimited) | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Tasks (unlimited) | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Calendar | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Time Tracking (basic) | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Habits | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Local storage | вң… | вң… | вң… | | **Privacy & Security** | | End-to-end encryption | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Local-first | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Manual export/import | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Auto-save (local folder) | вң… | вң… | вң… | | **Advanced Notes** | | Basic formatting | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Images & files | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Links & backlinks | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Graph view | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | Advanced search | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | Templates library | 3 basic | 50+ templates | Custom | | Custom CSS/themes | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | **Collaboration** | | Share read-only links | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Real-time collaboration | вқҢ | вң… (2-5 users) | вң… (unlimited) | | Team workspaces | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… | | Comments & @mentions | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | Permissions management | вқҢ | Basic | Advanced | | **Integrations** | | Calendar sync | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | Task integrations | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | Zapier/Make | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | API access | вқҢ | Read-only | Full access | | **Advanced Features** | | Billable hours | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | Invoice generation | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | Custom reports | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | AI features (BYOK) | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | **Enterprise** | | Team management | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… | | SSO (SAML/OAuth) | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… | | Audit logs | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… | | Self-hosted option | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… | | SOC 2 compliance | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… | | **Support** | | Community (GitHub) | вң… | вң… | вң… | | Email support | вқҢ | < 24hr | < 4hr | | Priority bug fixes | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | | Video onboarding | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… | --- ## Security Review Process ### For Every PR (Pull Request): 1. **Automated Checks:** - [ ] npm audit (no high/critical vulnerabilities) - [ ] ESLint security rules pass - [ ] TypeScript strict mode (no any types for user input) - [ ] Bundle size check (< 200KB) 2. **Manual Review:** - [ ] Code review by another developer - [ ] Privacy implications assessed - [ ] Security checklist completed - [ ] UX tested on mobile + desktop 3. **Before Merge:** - [ ] All tests pass - [ ] Documentation updated - [ ] Changelog updated ### For Security-Sensitive Features (Encryption, Auth, etc.): - [ ] External security audit (hire penetration tester) - [ ] Threat modeling completed - [ ] Security documentation written - [ ] 90-day disclosure policy in place --- ## Enforcement **These principles are MANDATORY, not suggestions.** **If a feature violates these principles:** 1. It will not be merged 2. It must be redesigned 3. No exceptions for "speed" or "MVP" **Privacy, security, and beautiful UX are not negotiable.** --- ## Summary | Principle | Implementation | Enforcement | |-----------|----------------|-------------| | **Privacy First** | Local-first, E2EE, zero-data-host | Privacy checklist for every feature | | **Security First** | WebCrypto, CSP, input sanitization | Security review for every PR | | **Beautiful UX** | < 1s load, a11y, polish | UX checklist for every feature | | **Open Source** | MIT license, public code | All code on GitHub | | **Free Core Features** | Notes, tasks, calendar, encryption | Never paywall core productivity | | **Pro Gating** | Advanced features only | Local license keys (privacy-respecting) | --- **Related:** - [Privacy Model](../security_privacy/privacy_model.md) - Zero-data-host architecture - [Feature Roadmap](../product/feature_roadmap.md) - Free vs Pro features timeline - [Business Model](../business/business_model.md) - Monetization strategy - [Implementation Plan](implementation_plan.md) - Sprint-by-sprint security requirements - [Sidebar Component Guide](sidebar_component_guide.md) - Standard patterns for navigation items - [Widget Development Guide](widget_development_guide.md) - Dashboard widget patterns **Document Status:** рҹ”’ MANDATORY **Last Updated:** November 13, 2025 **Applies To:** All features, all code, all decisions **Review Frequency:** Quarterly

   354вҶ’### 2.18 Thinking Framework Commands: /consider:*
   355вҶ’
   356вҶ’All thinking framework commands follow the pattern: `/consider:<framework> [topic or leave blank for current context]`
   357вҶ’
   358вҶ’#### 2.18.1 /consider:10-10-10
   359вҶ’
   360вҶ’- **Mental model**: Evaluate decisions across three time horizons (10 minutes, 10 months, 10 years)
   361вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   362вҶ’  - Architecture decisions with long-term implications
   363вҶ’  - Technology choices
   364вҶ’  - Feature prioritization
   365вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:10-10-10 Should we use IndexedDB or SQLite for persistence?`
   366вҶ’
   367вҶ’---
   368вҶ’
   369вҶ’#### 2.18.2 /consider:5-whys
   370вҶ’
   371вҶ’- **Mental model**: Drill to root cause by asking "why" repeatedly (Toyota's root cause analysis)
   372вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   373вҶ’  - Bug investigation
   374вҶ’  - Understanding why problems keep recurring
   375вҶ’  - Finding root causes vs. symptoms
   376вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:5-whys Why do users keep losing unsaved work?`
   377вҶ’
   378вҶ’---
   379вҶ’
   380вҶ’#### 2.18.3 /consider:eisenhower-matrix
   381вҶ’
   382вҶ’- **Mental model**: Apply Eisenhower matrix (urgent/important) to prioritize tasks or decisions
   383вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   384вҶ’  - Task prioritization
   385вҶ’  - Feature backlog triage
   386вҶ’  - Time management decisions
   387вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:eisenhower-matrix` (analyzes current todo list)
   388вҶ’
   389вҶ’---
   390вҶ’
   391вҶ’#### 2.18.4 /consider:first-principles
   392вҶ’
   393вҶ’- **Mental model**: Break down to fundamentals and rebuild from base truths (Elon Musk's approach)
   394вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   395вҶ’  - Challenging assumptions
   396вҶ’  - Innovative solutions
   397вҶ’  - Questioning "we've always done it this way"
   398вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:first-principles How should we implement real-time collaboration?`
   399вҶ’
   400вҶ’---
   401вҶ’
   402вҶ’#### 2.18.5 /consider:inversion
   403вҶ’
   404вҶ’- **Mental model**: Solve problems backwards - what would guarantee failure? (Charlie Munger's approach)
   405вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   406вҶ’  - Risk identification
   407вҶ’  - Avoiding pitfalls
   408вҶ’  - Defensive design
   409вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:inversion How could we make the authentication system completely insecure?`
   410вҶ’
   411вҶ’---
   412вҶ’
   413вҶ’#### 2.18.6 /consider:occams-razor
   414вҶ’
   415вҶ’- **Mental model**: Find simplest explanation that fits all the facts
   416вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   417вҶ’  - Choosing between complex architectures
   418вҶ’  - Bug hypothesis testing
   419вҶ’  - Avoiding over-engineering
   420вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:occams-razor Why is the widget rendering slowly?`
   421вҶ’
   422вҶ’---
   423вҶ’
   424вҶ’#### 2.18.7 /consider:one-thing
   425вҶ’
   426вҶ’- **Mental model**: Identify the single highest-leverage action (from "The ONE Thing" by Gary Keller)
   427вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   428вҶ’  - Sprint planning
   429вҶ’  - Focus during overwhelming workload
   430вҶ’  - Finding the critical path
   431вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:one-thing What's the one thing I can do to improve performance?`
   432вҶ’
   433вҶ’---
   434вҶ’
   435вҶ’#### 2.18.8 /consider:opportunity-cost
   436вҶ’
   437вҶ’- **Mental model**: Analyze what you give up by choosing this option
   438вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   439вҶ’  - Resource allocation
   440вҶ’  - Feature prioritization
   441вҶ’  - Technology adoption
   442вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:opportunity-cost Should we build custom analytics or integrate a library?`
   443вҶ’
   444вҶ’---
   445вҶ’
   446вҶ’#### 2.18.9 /consider:pareto
   447вҶ’
   448вҶ’- **Mental model**: Apply Pareto's principle (80/20 rule) to analyze arguments or current discussion
   449вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   450вҶ’  - Optimization efforts
   451вҶ’  - Bug fix prioritization
   452вҶ’  - Feature impact analysis
   453вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:pareto Which 20% of features drive 80% of user value?`
   454вҶ’
   455вҶ’---
   456вҶ’
   457вҶ’#### 2.18.10 /consider:second-order
   458вҶ’
   459вҶ’- **Mental model**: Think through consequences of consequences
   460вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   461вҶ’  - Architecture decisions
   462вҶ’  - API design
   463вҶ’  - Breaking changes
   464вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:second-order What happens if we make this field required?`
   465вҶ’
   466вҶ’---
   467вҶ’
   468вҶ’#### 2.18.11 /consider:swot
   469вҶ’
   470вҶ’- **Mental model**: Map strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats
   471вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   472вҶ’  - Technology evaluation
   473вҶ’  - Competitive analysis
   474вҶ’  - Strategic planning
   475вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:swot Using React Server Components for Neuman Brain`
   476вҶ’
   477вҶ’---
   478вҶ’
   479вҶ’#### 2.18.12 /consider:via-negativa
   480вҶ’
   481вҶ’- **Mental model**: Improve by removing rather than adding (Nassim Taleb's approach)
   482вҶ’- **Ideal scenarios**:
   483вҶ’  - Refactoring
   484вҶ’  - Simplification
   485вҶ’  - Removing technical debt
   486вҶ’- **Example**: `/consider:via-negativa How can we simplify the widget configuration API?`
   487вҶ’

   540вҶ’## 4. Workflows & Patterns
   541вҶ’
   542вҶ’### 4.1 Workflow: Claude-to-Claude Pipeline
   543вҶ’
   544вҶ’- **Steps**:
   545вҶ’  1. Identify complex, multi-stage task
   546вҶ’  2. Use `/create-meta-prompt` to create optimized pipeline prompt
   547вҶ’  3. Execute research stage вҶ’ produces analysis
   548вҶ’  4. Execute planning stage вҶ’ consumes analysis, produces plan
   549вҶ’  5. Execute implementation stage вҶ’ consumes plan, produces code
   550вҶ’- **Features involved**: create-meta-prompts skill, /create-meta-prompt command, /run-prompt command
   551вҶ’- **Use case**: Large features requiring research, architecture planning, and phased implementation
   552вҶ’
   553вҶ’---
   554вҶ’
   555вҶ’### 4.2 Workflow: Plan-Driven Development
   556вҶ’
   557вҶ’- **Steps**:
   558вҶ’  1. Use `/create-plan` to create hierarchical project plan
   559вҶ’  2. Plan includes briefs, roadmaps, phases, and tasks
   560вҶ’  3. Use `/run-plan` to execute the plan
   561вҶ’  4. Track progress with `/check-todos`
   562вҶ’  5. Use `/whats-next` for context handoff between phases
   563вҶ’- **Features involved**: create-plans skill, /create-plan, /run-plan, /check-todos, /whats-next
   564вҶ’- **Use case**: Structured development of complex features or multi-phase projects
   565вҶ’
   566вҶ’---
   567вҶ’
   568вҶ’### 4.3 Workflow: Task Delegation & Context Management
   569вҶ’
   570вҶ’- **Steps**:
   571вҶ’  1. Identify task suitable for delegation (clear scope, independent)
   572вҶ’  2. Use `/create-prompt` to create executable prompt
   573вҶ’  3. Use `/run-prompt` to execute in fresh context (parallel or sequential)
   574вҶ’  4. Main context remains clean, avoids token bloat
   575вҶ’  5. Results integrated back into main context
   576вҶ’- **Features involved**: /create-prompt, /run-prompt, /clear
   577вҶ’- **Use case**: Maintaining clean context while executing multiple independent tasks
   578вҶ’
   579вҶ’---
   580вҶ’
   581вҶ’### 4.4 Workflow: Session Handoff
   582вҶ’
   583вҶ’- **Steps**:
   584вҶ’  1. Approaching token limit or natural breakpoint
   585вҶ’  2. Use `/whats-next` to analyze current state and create handoff document
   586вҶ’  3. Use `/clear` to start fresh session
   587вҶ’  4. Resume with handoff document as context
   588вҶ’- **Features involved**: /whats-next, /clear
   589вҶ’- **Use case**: Long-running work across multiple sessions, context hygiene
   590вҶ’
   591вҶ’---
   592вҶ’
   593вҶ’### 4.5 Workflow: Structured Decision Making
   594вҶ’
   595вҶ’- **Steps**:
   596вҶ’  1. Identify decision or problem requiring analysis
   597вҶ’  2. Select appropriate `/consider:*` framework:
   598вҶ’     - Strategic/long-term вҶ’ `/consider:10-10-10`
   599вҶ’     - Root cause вҶ’ `/consider:5-whys`
   600вҶ’     - Prioritization вҶ’ `/consider:eisenhower-matrix`
   601вҶ’     - Innovation вҶ’ `/consider:first-principles`
   602вҶ’     - Risk вҶ’ `/consider:inversion`
   603вҶ’     - Simplicity вҶ’ `/consider:occams-razor`
   604вҶ’     - Focus вҶ’ `/consider:one-thing`
   605вҶ’     - Tradeoffs вҶ’ `/consider:opportunity-cost`
   606вҶ’     - Optimization вҶ’ `/consider:pareto`
   607вҶ’     - Consequences вҶ’ `/consider:second-order`
   608вҶ’     - Evaluation вҶ’ `/consider:swot`
   609вҶ’     - Simplification вҶ’ `/consider:via-negativa`
   610вҶ’  3. Apply framework to get structured analysis
   611вҶ’  4. Use insights to inform decision
   612вҶ’- **Features involved**: All 12 /consider:* commands
   613вҶ’- **Use case**: Architecture decisions, feature prioritization, problem-solving, strategic planning
   614вҶ’
   615вҶ’---
   616вҶ’
   617вҶ’### 4.6 Workflow: Debugging Investigation
   618вҶ’
   619вҶ’- **Steps**:
   620вҶ’  1. Encounter complex or persistent bug
   621вҶ’  2. Try standard debugging (logs, reproduction, hypothesis testing)
   622вҶ’  3. If unsuccessful, use `/debug` or `debug-like-expert` skill
   623вҶ’  4. Methodical investigation with evidence gathering
   624вҶ’  5. Hypothesis testing with rigorous verification
   625вҶ’  6. Root cause identification and fix
   626вҶ’- **Features involved**: debug-like-expert skill, /debug command, /consider:5-whys
   627вҶ’- **Use case**: Complex bugs, intermittent issues, systematic root cause analysis
   628вҶ’
   629вҶ’---
   630вҶ’
   631вҶ’### 4.7 Workflow: Extensibility Development
   632вҶ’
   633вҶ’- **Steps**:
   634вҶ’  1. Identify need for custom skill, command, or agent
   635вҶ’  2. Use appropriate creation command:
   636вҶ’     - `/create-agent-skill` for skills
   637вҶ’     - `/create-slash-command` for commands
   638вҶ’     - `/create-subagent` for agents
   639вҶ’     - `/create-hook` for event-driven automation
   640вҶ’  3. Implement following best practices from tache guidance
   641вҶ’  4. Use appropriate audit command:
   642вҶ’     - `/audit-skill` for skills
   643вҶ’     - `/audit-slash-command` for commands
   644вҶ’     - `/audit-subagent` for agents
   645вҶ’  5. Iterate based on audit findings
   646вҶ’  6. Commit with quality assured
   647вҶ’- **Features involved**: All create-* and audit-* commands
   648вҶ’- **Use case**: Extending Claude Code with project-specific capabilities
   649вҶ’
   650вҶ’---
   651вҶ’
   652вҶ’### 4.8 Workflow: Todo-Driven Task Management
   653вҶ’
   654вҶ’- **Steps**:
   655вҶ’  1. Use `/add-to-todos` to capture tasks as they arise
   656вҶ’  2. Use `/check-todos` to review and select next task
   657вҶ’  3. Execute task
   658вҶ’  4. Mark complete in TO-DOS.md
   659вҶ’  5. Repeat
   660вҶ’- **Features involved**: /add-to-todos, /check-todos
   661вҶ’- **Use case**: Lightweight task tracking without formal task files
   662вҶ’
